Section text:
通过混合式引导提升 D1留存率 >40%
传统城市建造类游戏易导致早期流失。首次用户
体验（ FTUE）应在前 15分钟内通过整合操作、
解谜或电影化时刻来提供强参与感，遵循经验证
的4X游戏基准。
2.

message.content:
This section outlines strategies to mitigate early player churn and improve Day 1 (D1) retention in city-building games. 
**Topics & Entities:** Game onboarding, D1 retention, FTUE (First Time User Experience), city-building games, 4X games.
**Claims & Metrics:** Traditional city-building games suffer from high early churn; implementing hybrid onboarding can increase D1 retention by >40%.
**Procedures & Timeframes:** FTUE should be structured within the first 15 minutes of gameplay, integrating core operations, puzzles, or cinematic moments to drive strong engagement, following proven 4X game benchmarks.
**Definitions:** FTUE = First Time User Experience.
**Answerable Questions:** How can early player loss be reduced in city-building games? What is the target metric for D1 retention improvement? What should FTUE include and how long should it last? Which industry design standards should onboarding follow?
**Tables, Figures & Evidence Types:** None present in this excerpt.

response_text:
This section outlines strategies to mitigate early player churn and improve Day 1 (D1) retention in city-building games. 
**Topics & Entities:** Game onboarding, D1 retention, FTUE (First Time User Experience), city-building games, 4X games.
**Claims & Metrics:** Traditional city-building games suffer from high early churn; implementing hybrid onboarding can increase D1 retention by >40%.
**Procedures & Timeframes:** FTUE should be structured within the first 15 minutes of gameplay, integrating core operations, puzzles, or cinematic moments to drive strong engagement, following proven 4X game benchmarks.
**Definitions:** FTUE = First Time User Experience.
**Answerable Questions:** How can early player loss be reduced in city-building games? What is the target metric for D1 retention improvement? What should FTUE include and how long should it last? Which industry design standards should onboarding follow?
**Tables, Figures & Evidence Types:** None present in this excerpt.
